column1: # Existing (9): basic_attack, thread_probe, minor_bind, compel_action, thread_assault, puppet_shield, network_bind, full_possession, the_hollow_practice
# Gaps filled: L1 core x5, L2 x2, L3 Architect x4, L3 Possessor x4, L3 Network x4, L5 Power x2, L9 Architect x2, L9 Possessor x2, L9 Network x2
# === L1 CORE (5 new, to reach 12 total) ===

column3: Yank a binding thread taut and vault along it. Move up to your speed to any space within line of sight of a creature you have bound; this movement doesn't provoke reactions.
Reaction: when an ally within 30 feet is targeted by an attack, pluck your strings. A bound creature you can see is forced to lurch into the attack's path, becoming the new target.
Passive: you are always aware of the direction and approximate distance of any creature you currently have bound, and whispers traveling your threads cannot be overheard by anyone else.

Send a quiet command down a binding thread to a creature you control. It takes one simple free-action instruction (drop item, speak a word, look left, open hand) at its next opportunity without its allies noticing.
Tighten every thread at once and drop them. All bound creatures take resonant damage equal to your SIGNAL modifier per bind on them; you release all your binds.
Pass your fingers through your open palm; the weave responds. The next bind you apply this turn counts as two stacks for the purpose of Thread Assault and Sever the Strings.
Unspool a length of thread you had hidden against your forearm. Gain one extra binding thread in reserve until your next long rest; binds you apply this turn cannot be dispelled by a single action.

Trace a tactical diagram in the air with a taut thread. Choose up to three creatures within 60 feet; until the start of your next turn, the first attack each of them makes is routed by your design and gains advantage.
Draw a thread between two bound creatures; they are now joined. Any forced movement, damage, or control effect applied to one is mirrored onto the other until one of them breaks free.
Reaction: when a bound creature makes an attack, you redirect the line. Change the attack's target to any other creature within the bound one's reach or range; the new target uses the original roll.

Lay an invisible lattice of threads across a 20-foot square you can see. The first enemy to enter or cross it becomes bound, takes a SIGNAL check penalty to move, and reveals its position to your whole network for one minute.

Slip a fraction of your will into a bound creature's off hand. Until the start of your next turn, as a free action once per turn you may use one of its non-magical items, weapons, or tools as if it were in your own hand.

Press your consciousness against a bound creature's features; its expressions, voice, and mannerisms become yours to puppet for one minute. Allies of the target treat its speech and gestures as trustworthy unless they witness a clear betrayal.

When a bound creature within sight would take damage, make a VEIL check. On a success, you shunt half of that damage into your own body through the thread; you gain advantage on your next attack against whoever struck them.

Send your mind down the thread and crouch behind a bound creature's eyes for a single round. You perceive as it perceives, may make one SIGNAL or VEIL check using its stats, and return knowing one truth it knew.

The network sees as one. Until the end of your next turn, you and every creature currently bound to you share line of sight; none of you can be flanked, surprised, or affected by illusions while a single member resists them.

The strings run both ways; you feel it tonight. When a creature bound to you takes damage, take half of that damage yourself; your next card this turn costs 1 less AP and adds that damage to its effect.

Speak a single order through every thread at once. All bound creatures immediately take the same short action on their initiative (move 10 feet, draw a weapon, stand up, or strike the nearest non-bound enemy).

Your network's minds braid into a single weave. Until the start of your next turn, any ally connected to your threads may use your SIGNAL modifier in place of their own on one check or save; you take 1 resonant damage each time they do.

For one minute, any creature that attacks you or an ally within 20 feet has a binding thread immediately attach to them; each attached thread imposes a penalty to their next attack and lets you apply a free Minor Bind on your turn.

Raise your hands like a conductor. For one minute, on your turn you may spend 1 AP to have every bound creature immediately make one basic attack against a target of your choice; the first chorus each round is free.

Unroll a loom of light from your palm and hang the battlefield from it. For one minute, every enemy within 60 feet counts as lightly bound by you; you may direct any two of them to act against each other at the start of each of your turns.

Write the next minute of battle into a living diagram. Once per round, before any attack roll you can see, you may declare a predetermined outcome (hit, miss, critical miss) from your plan; the creator's draw rules before dice.

You vacate your body and move into a bound creature entire. For one minute, you control it as yourself; your own body falls senseless but breathing. If the host dies, you return with one level of exhaustion and its final memory.

Sleeve yourself into up to three bound creatures at once; your will is in each like a candle in a lantern. On each of your turns, you may take one action through each host, and no host knows which hand is yours.

The network wakes as one mind. For one minute, you and every bound creature share hit points as a single pool, may use each other's reactions, and any damage dealt to one is divided evenly across the whole network.

The strings pull back through you. Each bound creature may spend their reaction to pour a portion of their strength into you; for each that does, gain temporary HP equal to their level and add their best attack modifier to yours for one round.
